html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 先正有言曰 - Classical formula meaning 'the ancients once said,' introducing a traditional saying.
  • 读书忌老，著书忌早 - Proverb meaning 'reading should be avoided in old age, and writing should be avoided in youth,' advising against certain activities at specific life stages.
  • 鼎既服膺而敢违之乎 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Having accepted this advice, how dare I go against it?' expressing the author's commitment to following the advice.
  • 缘鼎少膺孱弱，壮值乱离 - Phrase meaning 'Because I was weak in my youth and faced turmoil in my prime,' providing background on the author's life.
  • 濒于死者再，而皆得不死 - Phrase meaning 'I nearly died twice, but survived both times,' emphasizing the author's resilience.
  • 先慈诞鼎之前夕 - Phrase meaning 'The night before my mother gave birth to me,' setting the scene for a significant event in the author's life.
  • 梦一道者来叩首滕下 - Phrase meaning 'Dreamed of a Taoist coming to bow at the door,' describing a dream that foreshadows the author's birth.
  • 口不茹荤者十九年 - Phrase meaning 'Abstained from meat for nineteen years,' indicating the author's strict vegetarian diet.
  • 性好佛老 - Phrase meaning 'Having a fondness for Buddhism and Taoism,' describing the author's religious inclinations.
  • 闻人有谈玄者，听之忘倦 - Phrase meaning 'When I hear people talking about the profound, I listen without tiring,' showing the author's interest in deep philosophical discussions.
  • 尤爱仆媪说果报鬼怪逸事 - Phrase meaning 'Especially loving stories told by servants and maids about karma, ghosts, and strange events,' indicating the author's interest in supernatural tales.
  • 十五解为文 - Phrase meaning 'At fifteen, I began to write essays,' marking the author's entry into literary writing.
  • 旋得《感应编图说》读之，获瘳 - Phrase meaning 'Soon after, I read *Induction Compilation with Illustrations* and recovered,' showing the healing power of literature.
  • 二十外先慈见背，先嗣父广文公又见背 - Phrase meaning 'After twenty, my mother passed away, and my stepfather, the scholar, also passed away,' indicating the loss of important figures in the author's life.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 先正 - Refers to respected predecessors or sages.
  • 佛老 - Refers to Buddhism and Taoism, two major philosophical and religious traditions in Chinese culture.
  • 谈玄 - Refers to discussions of the profound or metaphysical, often associated with Taoist philosophy.
  • 仆媪 - Refers to servants and maids, who were common storytellers in traditional Chinese households.
  • 果报 - Refers to the concept of karma and retribution, central to Buddhist and Taoist beliefs.
  • 鬼怪逸事 - Refers to stories about ghosts, spirits, and unusual events, popular in traditional Chinese folklore.
  • 扁额、屏幛 - Refers to plaques, screens, and banners, common decorative items in traditional Chinese homes.
  • 咯血疾 - Refers to hemoptysis, a condition characterized by coughing up blood.
  • 感应编图说 - Refers to a book that combines text and illustrations to explain the principles of induction and causality.
  • 先慈 - Refers to the author's mother.
  • 先嗣父广文公 - Refers to the author's stepfather, who held the title of 'Guangwen Gong' (a scholarly title).

Proper Nouns

  • 夜雨秋灯录 - The title of the work, which translates to 'Night Rain and Autumn Lamp Records.'
  • 宣鼎 - The author's name.
  • 感应编图说 - The title of the book that helped the author recover from illness.
  • 先慈 - The author's mother.
  • 先嗣父广文公 - The author's stepfather, who held the title of 'Guangwen Gong.'

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 家难既起，外侮乘之 - After family troubles arose, external attacks took advantage of the situation.
  • 袅獍成群 - Evil people gathered in groups.
  • 争噬吾肉 - They fiercely attacked my resources.
  • 家道遂中落 - The family's fortunes declined to a low point.
  • 年廿四遇歉岁 - At the age of twenty-four, I encountered a year of famine.
  • 独卧枯寺，中饿几毙 - I lay alone in a desolate temple, nearly dying from hunger.
  • 旋得金经《莲花经》讽之 - I soon obtained the precious Lotus Sutra and recited it.
  • 机始转 - My fate began to change.
  • 廿六奉生父命赘入外家 - At the age of twenty-six, I married into my mother's family at the command of my living father.
  • 明年突红巾至 - The following year, the Red Turban rebels arrived.
  • 携家窜东海 - I fled to the East Sea with my family.
  • 慷慨从军，几死锋镝 - I bravely joined the army and nearly died in battle.
  • 旋又回海上卖画供饘粥 - I soon returned to the sea and sold paintings to provide for my daily needs.
  • 年三十一始入当道幕司笔札 - At the age of thirty-one, I began to serve in an official capacity, drafting documents for the ruling party.
  • 三十五馆淮海，三十九游山左 - At the age of thirty-five, I resided in Huaihai, and at the age of thirty-nine, I traveled in Shandong.
  • 奔波寒涩 - I traveled frequently and lived in poverty.
  • 近于托钵矣 - I have nearly become like a monk begging for alms.
  • 明年入兖郡滋阳署 - The following year, I entered the office in Ziyang County, Yan Prefecture.
  • 郡有少陵台 - The prefecture has the Shao Ling Terrace.
  • 峨峨百尺，古墟也 - It is a tall, ancient ruin, a hundred feet high.
  • 时九月将近，友人约登台望曲阜 - At the time, the ninth month was approaching, and a friend invited me to climb the terrace and look toward Qufu.
  • 西风逼人，黄花满地 - The west wind was chilling, and the ground was covered with chrysanthemums.
  • 鼎偶问友曰 - 鼎偶 asked his friend.
  • “今日何日？”曰：“廿八。” - What day is today? The twenty-eighth.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 家难 - Refers to family troubles or calamities, which could include internal conflicts or external threats.
  • 外侮 - Refers to external aggression, possibly from neighboring states or invaders.
  • 袅獍 - A metaphor for evil or treacherous people, often used in classical Chinese literature to describe those who betray or harm others.
  • 金经《莲花经》 - The Lotus Sutra is a central text in Mahayana Buddhism, symbolizing enlightenment and spiritual transformation.
  • 红巾 - Refers to the Red Turban Rebellion, a major uprising against the Yuan Dynasty in the late 13th and early 14th centuries.
  • 东海 - Refers to the East Sea, likely a metaphorical or literal place of refuge.
  • 锋镝 - Refers to the sharpness of weapons, symbolizing the dangers of war.
  • 海上 - Refers to the sea, often used metaphorically to describe a place of exile or refuge.
  • 笔札 - Refers to the act of writing or drafting documents, often associated with bureaucratic work.
  • 淮海 - Refers to the Huaihai region, a historical area in China.
  • 山左 - Refers to the eastern part of China, specifically Shandong Province.
  • 托钵 - Refers to the practice of begging for alms, traditionally associated with Buddhist monks.
  • 兖郡滋阳署 - Refers to a specific administrative office in Ziyang County, Yan Prefecture.
  • 少陵台 - A famous terrace, likely named after the poet Du Fu (Du the Hermit), who was known as 'Shao Ling' (少陵).
  • 曲阜 - The hometown of Confucius, a significant cultural and historical site in China.
  • 西风 - Refers to the west wind, which in Chinese literature often symbolizes the arrival of autumn and the passage of time.
  • 黄花 - Refers to chrysanthemums, which are associated with autumn and resilience in Chinese culture.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 不禁大惊 - Literal translation: 'cannot help but be greatly surprised,' indicating a strong emotional reaction.
  • 大恸 - Literal translation: 'great sorrow' or 'deep grief,' often associated with mourning.
  • 行年已四十矣 - Literal translation: 'having reached the age of forty,' a common way to indicate age in Classical Chinese.
  • 沦落犹是乎 - Literal translation: 'still in a state of decline,' expressing a sense of disappointment or failure.
  • 归则僵卧不语 - Literal translation: 'upon returning, lying motionless and speechless,' describing a state of extreme emotional distress.
  • 精神倘侊 - Literal translation: 'spirit wandering,' indicating a state of confusion or disorientation.
  • 不知所云 - Literal translation: 'unable to express oneself,' describing a state of incoherence.
  • 病十五日 - Literal translation: 'suffering from illness for fifteen days,' indicating a prolonged period of sickness.
  • 忽蹶然起 - Literal translation: 'suddenly leapt up,' describing a sudden and unexpected action.
  • 裁笺为阄 - Literal translation: 'cutting paper into lots,' a metaphor for making random choices or decisions.
  • 仿稗官例 - Literal translation: 'following the example of unofficial histories,' referring to the style of writing that includes anecdotes and gossip.
  • 先书一百余目 - Literal translation: 'first wrote over a hundred items,' indicating a large number of entries or topics.
  • 每夕作文一篇，或两篇 - Literal translation: 'wrote one or two essays each night,' describing a consistent writing routine.
  • 患遂霍然 - Literal translation: 'the illness suddenly disappeared,' indicating a rapid recovery.
  • 解馆 - Literal translation: 'left the teaching position,' referring to the end of a teaching career.
  • 赁宅任城 - Literal translation: 'rented a house in Rencheng,' indicating a change of residence.
  • 售书卖画 - Literal translation: 'sold books and paintings,' describing a means of earning a living.
  • 甫稍有余 - Literal translation: 'just barely had some surplus,' indicating a modest financial situation.
  • 回秦邮游虎阜 - Literal translation: 'returned to Qinyou and visited Tiger Hill,' indicating a specific travel destination.
  • 计得文一百一十五篇 - Literal translation: 'calculated to have written one hundred and fifteen pieces,' indicating the total number of writings.
  • 皆初稿而未及修饰者 - Literal translation: 'all were first drafts and had not been revised,' indicating the rough state of the writings.
  • 客有索观既竟 - Literal translation: 'when guests asked to see them and had finished reading,' indicating a review process.
  • 太早 - Literal translation: 'too early,' expressing a critical opinion.
  • 余深凛忌早之诫 - Literal translation: 'I deeply respect the warning against rushing,' indicating a cautious attitude.
  • 刀圭也 - Literal translation: 'a small measure,' often used metaphorically to describe something small or insignificant.
  • 未敢以著述论 - Literal translation: 'I dare not consider them as formal works,' indicating humility in self-assessment.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 贱辰 - Refers to a person's birthday, often used in a humble or self-deprecating manner.
  • 任城 - A place name, likely referring to a specific location in China.
  • 秦邮 - A place name, likely referring to a specific location in China.
  • 虎阜 - A place name, likely referring to Tiger Hill, a famous scenic spot in China.
  • 稗官 - Refers to unofficial historians or chroniclers, often associated with the *Wenxian Tongkao* (Comprehensive Examination of Literature and History).

Proper Nouns

  • 鼎 - A personal name or nickname, likely referring to the author or a character in the text.
  • 任城 - A place name, likely referring to a specific location in China.
  • 秦邮 - A place name, likely referring to a specific location in China.
  • 虎阜 - A place name, likely referring to Tiger Hill, a famous scenic spot in China.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 客问 - Formal expression meaning 'a guest asked.'
  • 夜雨秋灯 - Poetic phrase meaning 'night rain and autumn lamp,' evoking a melancholic and introspective mood.
  • 愁霖滴沥 - Describes the sound of rain falling, suggesting sorrow or melancholy.
  • 冷焰动摇 - Refers to the flickering of a cold flame, symbolizing instability or uncertainty.
  • 千里家山 - Means 'thousands of miles of homeland,' evoking a sense of longing or nostalgia.
  • 时入梦寐 - Means 'often entering dreams,' suggesting that the homeland is a recurring theme in the dreamer's thoughts.
  • 秋魂欲语 - Means 'the autumn soul wants to speak,' implying a desire to express deep feelings or thoughts.
  • 病魔乍来 - Means 'the disease demon suddenly comes,' indicating a sudden onset of illness or distress.
  • 无可奈何之境 - Means 'a state of helplessness,' describing a situation where one feels powerless.
  • 奋笔直书 - Means 'to write with great effort,' suggesting a strong emotional drive to express oneself.
  • 甲戌之冬 - Refers to the winter of the year Jia Xu, a specific time period.
  • 仙蝶过访 - Means 'a fairy butterfly visited,' a metaphorical expression suggesting a visit from a supernatural being.
  • 设乩于室 - Means 'to set up a divination board in the room,' a practice involving spiritual communication.
  • 子来前 - Means 'come here,' a command or invitation.
  • 子前身 - Means 'your previous life,' referring to a past incarnation or identity.
  • 罗浮冲虚观 - Refers to the Luofu Chongxu Temple, a specific place.
  • 弄笔头 - Means 'to play with the pen,' a metaphor for writing or literary activity.
  • 仙人序 - Means 'a preface by a fairy,' suggesting a divine or supernatural introduction.
  • 樵歌牧唱 - Means 'songs of woodcutters and shepherds,' representing simple, rural life.
  • 鬼董孤谐 - Means 'ghosts and spirits that are harmonious,' suggesting a peaceful coexistence with supernatural beings.
  • 尘之封，蠹之蚀 - Means 'dust covers and worms eat,' describing the decay of written works.
  • 忍乎哉 - Means 'how can one endure this?' expressing a sense of resignation or acceptance.
  • 时光绪三年春二月花朝日 - Refers to the day of the flower festival in the second month of spring in the third year of the Guangxu era.
  • 天长宣鼎瘦梅 - Refers to the author, Tianchang Xuanding Shoumei.
  • 自序于仙蝶来馆 - Means 'self-introduction at the Fairy Butterfly Pavilion,' indicating the place where the author wrote this.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 夜雨秋灯 - A common theme in Chinese literature, often associated with loneliness and introspection.
  • 罗浮冲虚观 - A real temple in Guangdong, known for its historical and cultural significance.
  • 甲戌之冬 - A specific time period in the Chinese calendar, which can be dated to 1874.
  • 仙蝶 - A metaphor for a supernatural being, often used in Chinese literature to represent a visit from a spirit or deity.
  • 设乩于室 - A traditional Chinese practice involving divination and spiritual communication.
  • 时光绪三年春二月花朝日 - A specific date in the Chinese calendar, corresponding to March 15, 1877.
  • 天长宣鼎瘦梅 - The author's name, indicating his identity and possibly his style or reputation.
  • 仙蝶来馆 - A specific place where the author wrote this, adding a personal touch to the text.

Proper Nouns

  • 客 - The guest asking the question.
  • 夜雨秋灯 - The poetic phrase being explained.
  • 罗浮冲虚观 - The Luofu Chongxu Temple.
  • 甲戌 - The year 1874.
  • 仙蝶 - The fairy butterfly.
  • 设乩于室 - The practice of setting up a divination board.
  • 时光绪三年春二月花朝日 - March 15, 1877.
  • 天长宣鼎瘦梅 - The author's name.
  • 仙蝶来馆 - The location where the author wrote this.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 以字行 - Means 'to be known by his courtesy name' or 'to be addressed by his courtesy name.'
  • 幼随父琥宦于粤 - Means 'as a young boy, he followed his father, who was an official in Guangdong.'
  • 清廉，穷其橐 - Means 'he was clean and honest, but his purse was empty.'
  • 母先逝，父继卒 - Means 'his mother died first, followed by his father.'
  • 亏库款，将系狱 - Means 'he incurred a debt to the treasury and was about to be imprisoned.'
  • 素审叔璧幕于苏 - Means 'he had always known that his uncle served in a government office in Suzhou.'
  • 欲往依之 - Means 'he wanted to go and rely on him.'
  • 潜焚两亲骸，以竹笼负之，徒步逸 - Means 'he secretly burned the remains of his parents, placed them in a bamboo cage, and carried them away on foot.'
  • 餐风咽露，跋涉奔波 - Means 'he ate wind and drank dew, traveling arduously.'
  • 寻叔无耗 - Means 'he searched for his uncle but found no trace of him.'
  • 时苏正岁歉，益困急 - Means 'at that time, Suzhou was suffering from a poor harvest, and he became even more desperate.'
  • 倾守囊钱，购半亩地，瘗之，志以碑，结茅若团瓢，守其侧 - Means 'he spent the last of his money to buy half an acre of land, buried the remains, marked the site with a stone tablet, and built a thatched hut nearby to guard it.'
  • 蓬头垢面，沦入乞儿，剩饭残羹，尚知荐祭 - Means 'he became a beggar, unkempt and dirty, but still knew to offer leftover food and soup as sacrifices.'
  • 闻吴儿山歌，学之，即当伍氏箫，始免馁 - Means 'he heard the mountain songs of the Wu people, learned them, and played the flute of the Wu clan, thus avoiding starvation.'
  • 朝村暮郭，倏忽三年 - Means 'he lived in the village in the morning and the town in the evening, and so it was three years.'
  • 偶倚古寺门，向阳扪虱 - Means 'one day, he leaned against the door of an ancient temple, facing the sun, and picked lice from his clothes.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 南宫认庵 - The name of the person being described, with '认庵' being his courtesy name.
  • 粤 - Refers to Guangdong, a province in southern China.
  • 清廉 - A virtue emphasizing integrity and honesty.
  • 橐 - Refers to a purse or bag.
  • 叔璧幕于苏 - Refers to the uncle serving in a government office in Suzhou.
  • 竹笼 - A bamboo cage, commonly used for carrying items.
  • 岁歉 - Refers to a poor harvest, indicating a time of famine or scarcity.
  • 倾守囊钱 - Means to spend all the money one has left.
  • 瘗之 - To bury the remains.
  • 志以碑 - To mark the site with a stone tablet.
  • 结茅若团瓢 - To build a thatched hut, with '团瓢' referring to a simple, round hut.
  • 蓬头垢面 - Describes someone who is unkempt and dirty.
  • 吴儿山歌 - Refers to the mountain songs of the Wu people, a regional cultural reference.
  • 伍氏箫 - Refers to the flute of the Wu clan, a musical instrument associated with the Wu people.
  • 古寺 - An ancient temple, a common place of refuge and reflection in Chinese culture.

Proper Nouns

  • 南宫认庵 - The name of the person being described.
  • 粤 - The place name Guangdong.
  • 叔璧 - The name of the boy's uncle.
  • 苏 - The place name Suzhou.
  • 竹笼 - A specific type of bamboo cage.
  • 吴儿山歌 - The mountain songs of the Wu people.
  • 伍氏箫 - The flute of the Wu clan.
  • 古寺 - An ancient temple.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 面即贵家园 - Facing the noble garden; indicates a location.
  • 时见美人楼上眺 - Often see a beautiful woman gazing from the upper floor; describes a recurring scene.
  • 顷一小环，艳年二八 - A moment later, a young woman of sixteen or seventeen; refers to a young and attractive woman.
  • 出而反掩其门 - She came out and then closed the door behind her; describes her actions.
  • 行向西 - She walked westward; indicates her direction.
  • 忽伏草际 - Suddenly crouched in the grass; describes an unexpected action.
  • 少时，整衣去 - After a while, she combed her clothes and left; describes her subsequent actions.
  • 知为小遗 - It was known to be a small gift; refers to the object she left behind.
  • 甫数步，即若有物坠地 - Just a few steps away, something seemed to fall to the ground; describes an event.
  • 软无声 - Soft and without sound; describes the nature of the fallen object.
  • 呼告之，婢不觉 - Called out to her, the maid did not notice; describes the reaction of others.
  • 遂掩襟往观 - Then covered her chest and went to look; describes her actions.
  • 锦袱也 - It was a brocade cloth; identifies the object.
  • 中裹金玉钗钏，珠宝零星 - Inside wrapped with gold and jade hairpins and bracelets, scattered with jewels; describes the contents of the brocade cloth.
  • 媵以鱼函，折作方胜 - Accompanied by a fish-shaped box, folded into a square pattern; describes additional items.
  • 书云 - The letter said; introduces the content of the letter.
  • 十郎哥哥足下 - Dear Brother Shilang; addresses the recipient.
  • 妹谬以陋质，获配清芬 - Sister, with my humble qualities, I was fortunate to be matched with your noble fragrance; expresses gratitude and humility.
  • 亲上做亲，幸中之幸 - A match made by parents, a happiness within happiness; emphasizes the significance of the marriage.
  • 牵牛西北，一水盈盈 - The cowherd in the northwest, a clear stream flows; uses a classical allusion to describe a romantic scene.
  • 孔雀东南，双声隐隐 - Peacocks in the southeast, their sounds faintly heard; uses another classical allusion to describe a romantic scene.
  • 盟深金石，妹喜嫁得梁鸿 - Our vows are as firm as stone, sister, I am delighted to have married a man like Liang Hong; expresses deep commitment and satisfaction.
  • 劫转沧桑，郎忽贫如司马 - The world has changed, and suddenly you are poor like Sima; expresses concern about the husband's financial situation.
  • 然而鲍宣对挽鹿车，阮氏何嫌犊鼻 - However, Bo Xuan pulled the ox cart, and the Ruan family had no objections to the ox cart; uses historical references to show acceptance of simplicity.
  • 幸鸡窗攻苦，卜鸿路飞腾 - May you study hard at the chicken window, and predict a soaring career; expresses hope for the future.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 美人 - Refers to a beautiful woman, often used in classical literature to describe an idealized female figure.
  • 锦袱 - A brocade cloth, often used as a decorative or functional item in classical settings.
  • 金玉钗钏 - Gold and jade hairpins and bracelets, symbols of wealth and beauty.
  • 鱼函 - A fish-shaped box, often used as a container for precious items.
  • 方胜 - A square pattern, often used in traditional Chinese designs.
  • 牵牛西北 - Refers to the legend of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl, a famous Chinese folktale.
  • 孔雀东南 - Refers to the legend of the Peacock and the Phoenix, another famous Chinese folktale.
  • 梁鸿 - A historical figure known for his virtuous character and simple lifestyle.
  • 司马 - A reference to Sima Qian, a famous historian who lived in poverty but achieved great fame.
  • 鲍宣 - A historical figure known for his simplicity and integrity.
  • 鸡窗 - A metaphor for a study or place of learning, often associated with刻苦 (hard work).

Proper Nouns

  • 十郎 - The name of the recipient of the letter.
  • 梁鸿 - A historical figure known for his virtuous character and simple lifestyle.
  • 司马 - A reference to Sima Qian, a famous historian.
  • 鲍宣 - A historical figure known for his simplicity and integrity.
  • 鸡窗 - A metaphor for a study or place of learning.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 敢怨标梅 - A classical expression meaning 'dare to blame the plum blossom,' a metaphor for unrequited love or longing.
  • 抚青春而未艾 - Means 'to cherish the prime of youth while it is still unexhausted,' emphasizing the beauty and vitality of youth.
  • 唯祈折桂 - Means 'only pray to pluck the osmanthus,' a metaphor for achieving success or glory.
  • 脱白袷以来迎 - Means 'to shed the white robe and come to welcome,' a metaphor for changing one's attire or status to greet someone.
  • 逆知青眼频更 - Means 'to anticipate the frequent change of favor,' a metaphor for the fickleness of favor or affection.
  • 红丝欲断 - Means 'the red thread is about to be severed,' a metaphor for the impending end of a relationship.
  • 每听狐语，似怨前度之冰 - Means 'to often hear the fox's words, seeming to complain about the previous ice,' a metaphor for hearing complaints about past coldness or indifference.
  • 欲倩蜂媒，再接他家之酒 - Means 'to wish to ask the bee as a matchmaker, to reconnect with another family's wine,' a metaphor for seeking a new connection or relationship.
  • 心石坚而不转 - Means 'the heart is as hard as stone and does not turn,' a metaphor for an unchanging or steadfast heart.
  • 辞簧鼓而须防 - Means 'to avoid the flattery of the reed pipe,' a metaphor for being cautious of flattery or deceit.
  • 若真挟瑟改弦 - Means 'if truly to take up the zither and change the strings,' a metaphor for making a significant change or turning a new page.
  • 定弹黄鹄 - Means 'to surely play the song of the yellow crane,' a metaphor for achieving a noble or lofty goal.
  • 时拟传笺布意 - Means 'to sometimes plan to convey one's intentions through letters,' a metaphor for expressing one's thoughts or feelings.
  • 恨少青鸾 - Means 'to regret the lack of the green phoenix,' a metaphor for the absence of a messenger or means of communication.
  • 不尽缠绵，泥中人再为哓舌 - Means 'the endless entanglement, the person in the mud speaks again,' a metaphor for the persistence of emotional entanglement.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sequence '女愤欲自缢，解带挂庭树，妪趋救而尼犹怒' describes the woman's emotional state, her attempt to commit suicide, and the old woman's attempt to save her, while the nun remains angry. The sentence is clear but requires careful translation to maintain the emotional tension.
  • The clause '妪亦怨曰：“老秃厮，乃徒以慈悲获罪责，然则定逐伊何处去？”' is a direct quote from the old woman, expressing her frustration and confusion. The sentence structure is clear but requires attention to the emotional tone.
  • The clause '尼曰：“听自便耳！”' is a short, direct response from the nun, indicating her decision to let the woman go her own way. The sentence is straightforward but requires attention to the nun's tone.
  • The clause '妪知女不愿回俗家，曰：“曷随老身去？”' describes the old woman's understanding of the woman's reluctance to return to her former life and her suggestion to go with her. The sentence structure is clear but requires attention to the emotional context.
  • The sequence '女犹踌躇，尼遽抚掌，曰：“妙哉妙哉！速去速去！”' describes the woman's hesitation, the nun's sudden approval, and her command for the woman to leave quickly. The sentence structure is clear but requires attention to the emotional dynamics.
  • The clause '立即驱出，掩双扉' describes the nun's action of expelling the woman and closing the doors. The sentence is straightforward but requires attention to the physical action.
  • The sequence '适杜正归来看妪，见妪携女至，惊询何来，详告所以' describes Du's arrival, his surprise at seeing the woman, and the woman's explanation. The sentence structure is clear but requires attention to the sequence of events.
  • The clause '女伏地涕泣呼杜叔，杜喜曰：“前云为佛郎作冰者，即此女耳！此中有天缘，幸勿错过！”' describes the woman's emotional reaction to seeing Du, Du's recognition of her, and his encouragement to follow the opportunity. The sentence structure is clear but requires attention to the emotional and narrative context.
  • The clause '约略告十九，即慨分游橐，代谋花烛俾合卺' describes Du's explanation, his generous act, and his efforts to arrange a marriage. The sentence structure is clear but requires attention to the narrative flow.

  • 遂赠以婢，且与数百金，俾理旧业焉 - He then gave her a maid and several hundred gold pieces, to help her manage her old business. '俾理旧业' (to help her manage her old business) suggests that the gift was intended to support her in continuing her previous occupation.
  • 明年，二子场乡捷 - The following year, his two sons passed the provincial exams. '场乡捷' (passed the provincial exams) refers to success in the imperial examinations, which were a crucial step in the Chinese civil service system.

  • 奉师命，坚请银夫人随喜 - Following the master's command, they firmly invited Lady Silver to join in the joy. '随喜' (join in the joy) is a polite way of inviting someone to participate in a celebration.
  • 且以旧犊鼻缄寄 - And sent an old sleeve as a token. '旧犊鼻缄寄' (sent an old sleeve as a token) refers to a small, personal gift, often given as a memento.
  • 此杜郎旧物也 - This is an old possession of Du Lang. '杜郎' (Du Lang) is a poetic way of referring to a man named Du, and '旧物' (old possession) indicates that the item has historical or sentimental value.
  • 瞑目将就涅盘 - Closing her eyes, she was about to enter nirvana. '瞑目将就涅盘' (closing her eyes, she was about to enter nirvana) refers to the moment of death, where the soul is said to enter a state of liberation.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '否則骈死荒郊，于大王毫无禅益' introduces a contrasting outcome and emphasizes the futility of the situation.
  • The sequence '捻大喜，赏其慧美，信其恳笃，乃舍众而随女以行' describes the bandits' reactions and actions, which must be read as a series of connected events.
  • The sequence '女慨然导，略反顾，以目示众，令遁，众始免脱焉' describes the girl's actions and the others' escape, which must be read as a continuous narrative.
  • The sequence '从行里许，至一极大村，诡云己宅，内外阒如，破键入，延贼坐厅事' describes the journey and the arrival at the village, which must be read as a series of connected events.
  • The long sentence '女拾地下蒲葵扇，且摇且语曰：“大王等长途跋涉，马足奔驰，饥渴甚矣。且少憩纳凉，待我诣内煮茗，略尽东道谊。然而具畚锸，先掘我家，再掘他家也。”' describes the girl's interaction with the bandits, which must be read as a continuous narrative.
  • The sequence '贼笑颔之。以为茕茕弱息，固已瓮鳖釜鱼，尚何外逸？解衣裸体，歌啸纵横' describes the bandits' reactions and behavior, which must be read as a series of connected events.
  • The sequence '日将堕崦嵫，瀹茗人仍不出，奔内搜阅，贼大骇，盖女已挂梁上，体冰而僵矣' describes the progression of events leading to the girl's death, which must be read as a continuous narrative.
  • The sequence '贼恶其诳己，思淫其尸。甫解下，一贼大呼倒地，视脑后若有锥击，顷刻毙' describes the bandits' reaction to the girl's death and the sudden and violent event, which must be read as a series of connected events.

  • 画则得所南瓮家法，更参以徐青藤老人 - Describes V.板桥先生's painting style, influenced by the South Well School and incorporating elements of Xu Gu.

  • 为秀才时，三至邗江，售书卖画，无识者，落拓可怜 - Describes V.板桥先生's early career, traveling to Yangzhou to sell calligraphy and paintings but unrecognized and living a poor and pitiful life.
  • 复举于乡，旋登甲榜，声名大震 - Describes V.板桥先生's success in the imperial examinations, passing provincial and metropolitan exams and gaining widespread fame.
  • 再至邗江，则争索先生墨妙者，户外履常满 - Describes the situation after V.板桥先生's success, where people in Yangzhou competed to buy his calligraphy and paintings, and his home was often crowded with visitors.

  • 时江西张真人入觐回，道出邗江，商人争媚之，欲得先生书联献真人 - Describes the situation where Zhang Zhenren, a Taoist master from Jiangxi, returned from an audience with the emperor, passing through Yangzhou, and local merchants competed to flatter him by seeking V.板桥先生's calligraphy couplets to present to him.

  • 一颦一笑，郎若师承之，而摹肖之，自有非常遇 - Means 'every frown and smile, if the young man imitates them as if he were learning from a master, he will have extraordinary luck.' This is a metaphorical way of saying that by imitating the speaker's behavior, the young man will achieve great success.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The long sentence '郎满身皆傲骨，即侥幸，亦必遭妒嫉，覆身家，况蠖屈家园，有不动辄得咎者乎' must be read as a series of interconnected clauses, each building on the previous one to create a coherent argument.
  • The clause '妾善媚者也，一颦一笑，郎若师承之，而摹肖之，自有非常遇' introduces the speaker's self-description and the advice she gives to the young man.
  • The clause '痴哉！郎也。枕席狎匿，亲近极矣。彼举师保者，先有畏心；学宠爱者，纯秉天性，故知其易也' provides a detailed explanation of the speaker's advice, using a series of rhetorical questions and statements.
  • The clause '学而不成，由于爱妾之不深，妾去矣，实不愿郎之偃蹇也' concludes the speaker's advice, expressing her frustration and concern.
  • The clause '善。从此步趋，渐能神化，一切笑言，无不解颐。女曰：可矣' describes the young man's progress and the speaker's satisfaction with his improvement.
  • The clause '试期迫，女为束装。生曰：如卿言，仆老不作茧，顷何劝驾之殷。曰：康了诚康了耳' sets the context for the upcoming event and the young man's response to the speaker's advice.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 措大眼孔中，真未曾有耳 - 措大 is a colloquial term for a scholar or literatus, often with a condescending tone. 眼孔 means 'eye socket' or 'perspective,' and 真未曾有耳 means 'truly something I have never seen before.' This phrase expresses amazement or disbelief.
  • 翌晤金，略询昨宵事 - 翌 means 'next day,' 晤 means 'meet,' and 略询 means 'briefly inquire.' This phrase indicates a meeting the next day to ask about the previous night's events.
  • 某之所乐，君亦有其乐，喋喋何为 - 某 is a humble self-reference, 所乐 means 'what I enjoy,' 君 is a respectful form of 'you,' 喋喋 means 'chattering' or 'talking incessantly.' This phrase suggests that the speaker finds joy in something, and the listener also finds joy, so why bother talking incessantly?
  • 虽然，当慎密，万勿为外人道也 - 虽然 means 'even though' or 'nevertheless,' 当慎密 means 'must be cautious,' 万勿 means 'never,' 为外人道 means 'tell outsiders.' This phrase emphasizes the need for secrecy.
  • 榜发出，果落孙山外 - 榜发 means 'the results are announced,' 果 means 'indeed,' 落孙山外 is an idiom meaning 'fail the exam' or 'not be selected.'
  • 抑郁无聊赖，对女唏嘘 - 抑郁 means 'depressed,' 无聊赖 means 'without purpose or direction,' 对女唏嘘 means 'to sigh in front of the woman.' This phrase describes the protagonist's state of mind and his reaction.
  • 郎勿戚戚，且市小肴馔，沽美酒，闭妾暗室，代烹调，延金啖，借以话别，或得将伯助 - 郎 is a term of endearment for a man, 勿戚戚 means 'do not be sad,' 且 means 'and,' 市 means 'buy,' 肴馔 means 'dishes,' 沽 means 'buy,' 美酒 means 'good wine,' 闭妾暗室 means 'close the dark room,' 代烹调 means 'prepare the food,' 延金啖 means 'invite Jin to eat,' 借以话别 means 'to say goodbye,' 或得将伯助 means 'perhaps get some help.' This phrase suggests a plan to have a farewell meal with Jin.
  • 落魄人，技只此耳 - 落魄人 means 'a person who is down and out,' 技只此耳 means 'my skills are only this.' This phrase expresses the protagonist's self-deprecating view of his abilities.
  • 纳粟，需多金，则未可咄嗟办 - 纳粟 means 'to offer grain as a tribute,' 需多金 means 'requires a lot of money,' 则未可咄嗟办 means 'cannot be accomplished in a hurry.' This phrase indicates that the task is too costly and cannot be done quickly.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 吴孝子 - Title 'Filial Son of Wu' reflects the high value placed on filial piety in Chinese culture.
  • 少孤 - Concept of being orphaned at a young age is a common theme in Chinese literature, often used to highlight personal virtues and resilience.
  • 喑哑不能言 - Condition of being mute and deaf is often associated with tragic fates in classical Chinese literature, but here it is used to emphasize the protagonist's unique character.
  • 凡病哑者，耳必聋 - Belief in the connection between muteness and deafness is rooted in traditional Chinese medical theory, which often attributed physical conditions to imbalances in the body.
  • 因贫，为邑之质库担水 - Occupation of a water carrier for a pawnshop is a common role for the poor in classical Chinese society, reflecting the economic conditions of the time.
  • 得值即奉母 - Act of giving all earnings to one's mother is a powerful demonstration of filial piety, a core virtue in Confucian ethics.
  • 不敢浪以一钱自啖嚼 - Extreme self-denial shown by the protagonist is a reflection of the high moral standards expected of filial children.
  • 虽哑且聋，而性敏 - Combination of physical limitations and mental acuity is a recurring theme in Chinese literature, often used to create compelling characters.
  • 能以己意揣母意 - Ability to understand one's mother's needs and desires is a key aspect of filial piety, and this skill is highly valued in Chinese culture.
  • 母亦习久，能以势与孝子言 - Mutual adaptation and communication between the mother and son is a touching example of familial love and understanding.
  • 每日，母思食何物，必呀呀请母命，而后诣郭购之回 - Daily routine of the protagonist highlights his diligence and care for his mother, reinforcing the theme of filial piety.
  • 如四指作圈，即知为饼 - Use of gestures to communicate specific foods is a practical solution to their communication barrier, demonstrating the ingenuity of the characters.
  • 撮指覆腕，即知为馒首 - Detailed description of gestures adds a layer of realism to the story, making the characters more relatable.
  • 又手成八字，即知为水角子 - Continuation of the gesture list further emphasizes the protagonist's intelligence and adaptability.
  • 伸掌使平，知为鱼 - Gestures for different foods show the protagonist's resourcefulness and his deep connection with his mother.
  • 垂手如提，知为肉之类 - Final gesture in the list completes the picture of the protagonist's daily life and his commitment to his mother.
  • 百无一爽 - Phrase 'not once was there a mistake' underscores the accuracy and reliability of the protagonist's actions.
  • 母老且病，每食若微且噎，孝子即于暗处洒眼泪 - Emotional depth of the story is revealed through the protagonist's silent tears, highlighting his deep love and concern for his mother.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '守妻本艳妾僭正位者，颇恶生，年余有妊，恐生他日碍真儿，益讽婢子小鹊谮生短' describes the wife's jealousy and actions against the son. The sentence structure is complex, with multiple clauses and actions.
  • The clause '惟延师授生举子业，勖最殷而功亦大进' shows the husband's support for the son's education and the son's progress.
  • The clause '时于阃内施恶声，渐自操仗挞假子，逐之者屡矣' describes the wife's increasingly harsh treatment of the son.
  • The clause '守度其终不相能，呼生于无人处，泣曰' shows the husband's decision to end the relationship with the son and his emotional farewell.
  • The clause '汝从来处来，仍从去处去，有千金相赠，了我父子缘' is a formal and emotional farewell, with the husband giving the son a significant sum of money to end their relationship.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The magistrate's anger grew more intense, and they all knelt and said. This clause introduces the magistrate's anger and the response of the people.
  • Please allow me to test the younger ones, and ensure that they will be captured and brought to the public court within the day. This clause outlines the proposed action.
  • He took a sedan chair to inspect the temples, and the bookcases were still there, but the person was nowhere to be found. This clause describes the inspection and the result.
  • The walls were covered with wet ink, and he left behind a poem saying. This clause introduces the poem.
  • One wave has just subsided, another rises. In the inn, I am often startled awake, unable to sleep. This clause describes the continuous trouble and the inability to sleep.
  • I look at the world with contempt, seeing its dangers. Having spent all my gold, I feel lighter. This clause expresses the speaker's disdain for the world and the feeling of freedom after spending all his wealth.
  • I am used to drowning my sorrows in alcohol. I avoid trouble, but it finds me anyway. This clause describes the speaker's habit of drinking and the inevitability of trouble.
  • Counting the days, my return should not be far off. There are suspicious troops beneath Mount Baxia. This clause expresses the speaker's anticipation of return and the presence of suspicious troops.
  • In the western border of the prefecture, ten miles away, there is originally Mount Baxia, an ancient site of Huainan. This clause describes the location and significance of Mount Baxia.
  • Reading the last line, I suspect he is hiding there. When I went to look, indeed, he was sitting cross-legged in a stone cave, stupid like a wooden chicken. This clause describes the discovery of the person in the cave.
  • He helped him return, and when asked, he remained silent. This clause describes the return and the person's silence.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This phrase introduces a trial method and its effectiveness.
  • This sequence describes contrasting weather conditions.

  • This concessive clause emphasizes the gentleman's kindness.
  • This sequence of events is tightly linked, describing the sudden interruption.
  • This sequence of actions is clear but rapid, emphasizing urgency.
  • This detailed description of the treatment process is clear.
  • This series of unexpected outcomes is vivid and dramatic.
  • This final statement summarizes the futility of the repeated attempts.
  • This final sequence of events adds a sense of isolation and urgency.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 俯首呵冻 - Literally means 'bending down and exhaling to warm oneself,' describing the act of warming oneself by breathing on cold hands or objects.
  • 极力熨贴 - Means 'to apply great effort to make something smooth or comfortable,' suggesting meticulous care.
  • 绝不为一文琐屑生恚怒 - Means 'not to be angered by even the smallest trivial matter.'
  • 大言之曰 - Means 'said loudly and boldly.'
  • 鄙哉伧也 - A derogatory expression meaning 'what a vulgar peasant!'
  • 药甚平淡 - Means 'the medicine is very plain or simple.'
  • 何子细乃尔 - Means 'why so meticulous?'
  • 仍更与之 - Means 'still gave it to him again.'
  • 视釜中药已竭 - Means 'saw that the medicine in the cauldron was exhausted.'
  • 狂笑 - Means 'laughed wildly.'
  • 声可震屋瓦 - Means 'the sound could shake the roof tiles.'
  • 袖出一钱掷釜中 - Means 'took out a coin from his sleeve and threw it into the cauldron.'
  • 以此聊酬一夜劳 - Means 'to pay for a night's labor with this.'
  • 踏雪竟去 - Means 'walked through the snow and left.'
  • 嵌一极古五铢钱 - Means 'embedded an extremely ancient five-zhu coin.'
  • 坚滞如铸 - Means 'as hard and solid as cast metal.'
  • 药气上腾，成五色香云 - Means 'the medicinal vapor rose up, forming a five-colored fragrant cloud.'
  • 氤氲不散者终夜 - Means 'lingered and did not disperse all night.'
  • 由此药更灵 - Means 'from this, the medicine became even more potent.'
  • 人疑丐者仙也 - Means 'people suspected that the beggar was a fairy.'
  • 无疾一笑逝 - Means 'died peacefully with a smile.'
  • 子孙读书，多有游庠者 - Means 'his descendants studied, and many of them became scholars.'
  • 犹悬壶市药为生 - Means 'still practiced medicine and sold herbs for a living.'
  • 世世保守釜炉如连城 - Means 'generation after generation preserved the cauldron and stove as if they were treasures worth a thousand cities.'
  • 余髫龄亲见之 - Means 'I personally saw it when I was a child.'
  • 釜者，古勺斗也 - Means 'the cauldron is an ancient ladle or measuring vessel.'
  • 炉者，小折脚铛也 - Means 'the stove is a small tripod pot.'
  • 忠魂入梦兖济道署 - Means 'the loyal spirit entered a dream at the Yanzhijiaodao office.'
  • 在兖郡城西，本明季都阃府旧址 - Means 'located west of the city of Yanzhou, originally the site of the Ming dynasty's military governor's residence.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 五铢钱 - A type of ancient Chinese coin from the Han dynasty.
  • 悬壶 - A metaphor for practicing medicine, derived from the legend of the immortal Hua Tuo who hung a gourd to signify his medical practice.
  • 游庠 - Refers to becoming a scholar or student at a school or academy.
  • 连城 - A metaphor for something extremely valuable, often used to describe precious objects or treasures.
  • 都阃府 - Refers to the military governor's residence during the Ming dynasty.
  • 兖济道署 - Refers to the administrative office of the Yanzhijiaodao region.

  • 悬壶 - A metaphor for practicing medicine.
  • 游庠 - Becoming a scholar or student.
  • 连城 - A metaphor for something extremely valuable.
  • 都阃府 - The military governor's residence during the Ming dynasty.
  • 兖济道署 - The administrative office of the Yanzhijiaodao region.

  • 徘徊中庭 - Wanders in the central courtyard.
  • 旋一禿髮童子，投刺入 - Suddenly, a bald-headed child enters with an introduction.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 子既作《夜雨秋灯录》 - Respectful term for a person, 'you' or 'sir'; 'having already written'; book title, which should be kept consistent in translation.
  • 纪其崖略 - 'to record' or 'to document'; 'briefly' or 'in outline'.
  • 俾后之宦游者 - 'to make' or 'to enable'; 'future' or 'subsequent'; officials traveling on official business.
  • 知此中有人 - 'to know'; 'there is someone here'.
  • 不致削 - 'not to the extent of' or 'not to the point of'; 'to diminish' or 'to reduce'.
  • 岂非笔墨缘欤 - 'is it not' or 'is it not that'; 'writing' or 'literature'; 'fate' or 'destiny'.
  • 余心虽应诺 - 'my heart' or 'my mind'; 'although' or 'even though'; 'to agree' or 'to promise'.
  • 且欲咨询 - 'and' or 'also'; 'to consult' or 'to inquire'.
  • 而口噤不能言一字 - 'but' or 'however'; 'to be unable to speak'; 'not able to utter a single word'.
  • 贵人旋起 - 'a noble person' or 'a distinguished individual'; 'to rise up' or 'to stand up'.
  • 余唯拜送 - 'I' or 'me'; 'only' or 'merely'; 'to bow and send off'.
  • 翌日，将遣人以名字相告 - 'the next day'; 'will send someone'; 'to inform with names'.
  • 可以略见一斑 - 'can' or 'may'; 'to see briefly'; 'a small part' or 'a glimpse'.
  • 寒泉百尺吐长虹 - 'cold spring'; 'a hundred feet'; 'to spew' or 'to emit'; 'a long rainbow'.
  • 多少风云在瓮中 - 'how many' or 'how much'; 'clouds and winds' (metaphorically, 'events' or 'changes'); 'in a jar' or 'in a container'.
  • 遗蜕纵教黄土压 - 'abandoned shell' or 'remains'; 'even if' or 'although'; 'pressed by yellow earth'.
  • 精灵已逐鼎湖龙 - 'spirit' or 'essence'; 'has followed'; 'legendary dragon associated with the Yellow Emperor'.
  • 回首燕台策马行 - 'to look back'; 'historical site, often associated with the Yan Platform'; 'to ride a horse'.
  • 征途顺访绿杨营 - 'journey' or 'campaign'; 'to visit'; 'place named Green Willow Camp'.
  • 惨闻帝抱虞渊痛 - 'to hear with sorrow'; 'the emperor's embrace'; 'legendary place of sorrow'.
  • 国破家亡敢再生 - 'the country is destroyed and the family is lost'; 'dare to be reborn'.
  • 爱妾随身字娘 - 'beloved concubine'; 'by her side'; 'her name is 娘'.
  • 一般殉节共流芳 - 'the same'; 'to sacrifice oneself for righteousness'; 'to share in the fragrance' or 'to be remembered together'.
  • 行人莫当胭脂井 - 'travelers'; 'do not mistake'; 'well associated with a famous concubine'.
  • 玉虎偷窥水尚香 - 'jade tiger'; 'to secretly peek'; 'the water still smells fragrant'.
  • 千古崇窿土一台 - 'throughout the ages'; 'high and majestic'; 'platform of earth'.
  • 金蚕飞出总堪哀 - 'golden silkworm'; 'to fly out'; 'always worthy of pity'.
  • 年年风雨清明节 - 'year after year'; 'wind and rain'; 'Qingming Festival'.
  • 若个梨花麦饭来 - 'which one' or 'which'; 'pear blossom'; 'wheat porridge'.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 何須短碣題名字 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Why need a short stone tablet to inscribe one's name?' Suggests a desire for immortality or recognition.
  • 杜甫南樓一散仙 - Refers to Du Fu, a famous Tang Dynasty poet, as a 'dispersed immortal' in the Southern Pavilion, implying his transcendence and poetic genius.
  • 吟已，回首顧余，揮手若示止步狀 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'After reciting, he turned around and looked at me, waving as if to indicate that I should stop.' Conveys a moment of interaction and instruction.
  • 余正惶惑，若足底誤踏蒼莓，一滑傾撲而醒，枕上默憶所吟，一字不爽 - Metaphorical description of the narrator's state of confusion and sudden awakening, with '苍莓' (wild strawberries) symbolizing something that causes a stumble.
  • 聽窗外風聲颼然，若吟韻猶在耳邊也 - Means 'Hearing the wind outside, it sounded like the recited verses were still in my ears.' Evokes a lingering memory of the poetic experience.
  • 謹志於懷，殊不可測 - Means 'I carefully recorded it in my heart, but it is deeply mysterious.' Indicates the narrator's attempt to preserve the experience but acknowledges its enigmatic nature.
  • 次夕，適道署幕府某君來，試以土阜問 - Means 'The next day, a certain gentleman from the provincial office came, and I tested him with the question about the earth mound.' Introduces a new character and a specific inquiry.
  • 某愀然曰 - Means 'He looked solemn and said,' indicating a serious or somber tone.
  • 其下有井，為明忠臣昆山曹公廷楨死難處也 - Means 'Below it is a well, where the loyal minister Cao Tingzhen of Kunshan died a heroic death.' Provides historical context and identifies a significant figure.
  • 甲申年，公正行取入都，道出此邦，訪友是署 - Means 'In the year Jia Shen, he was on his way to the capital, passing through this region to visit friends in the provincial office.' Gives the timeline and purpose of the event.
  • 忽偵隸報煤山之變，公撫膺大哭，曰：‘吾不忍事二主！’故縱身投井死 - Means 'Suddenly, a spy reported the chaos on Coal Mountain, and he clutched his chest and wept, saying, ‘I cannot serve two masters!’ So he threw himself into the well and died.' Describes the tragic event and the minister's noble decision.
  • 土人義之，遂閉塞井闌，上加黃土，因近官衙，不敢作殯宮墓道，然亦不忍再酌寒泉，遂築如平阜 - Means 'The locals honored him, so they sealed the well, covered it with yellow earth, and because it was near the government office, they did not dare to build a tomb, but they also could not bear to drink from the cold spring again, so they built it like a flat mound.' Explains the locals' actions and the transformation of the site.
  • 至道光某甲子，官此者某公，其妾素驕，亦河東怒獅也 - Means 'In the year Jia Zi of Daoguang, a certain official who was in charge here had a wife who was usually proud, like a lioness from Hedong.' Introduces a new character and describes her personality.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 杜甫 - A renowned Tang Dynasty poet, often referred to as the 'Poet Sage.'
  • 甲申年 - Refers to the year 1644, a significant year in Chinese history marking the fall of the Ming Dynasty and the rise of the Qing Dynasty.
  • 煤山之變 - Refers to the chaos and turmoil associated with the fall of the Ming Dynasty, particularly the suicide of the last Ming emperor on Coal Hill.
  • 昆山曹公廷楨 - Cao Tingzhen, a loyal official from Kunshan who died a heroic death during the transition from the Ming to the Qing Dynasty.
  • 河東怒獅 - A metaphor for a fierce and proud woman, often used to describe a strong-willed and dominant wife.

Proper Nouns

  • 杜甫 - Du Fu, a famous Tang Dynasty poet.
  • 昆山曹公廷楨 - Cao Tingzhen, a loyal official from Kunshan.
  • 甲申年 - The year 1644, marking the fall of the Ming Dynasty.
  • 煤山之變 - The chaos and turmoil associated with the fall of the Ming Dynasty.
  • 道光 - The reign title of Emperor Daoguang of the Qing Dynasty.
  • 河東怒獅 - A metaphor for a fierce and proud woman.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 大声曰 - A formal way of saying 'shouted loudly,' indicating a dramatic or emphatic statement.
  • 吾明季曹廷桢也 - A self-introduction in a formal, classical style, where '明季' refers to the late Ming dynasty.
  • 再问，而如君已苏 - A phrase indicating that the person was asked again, and the subject had already recovered.
  • 扶之上房，药饵始愈 - A sequence of actions: being helped to the upper room and finally recovering after taking medicine.
  • 淫威稍杀 - A phrase meaning 'the tyrannical power was somewhat diminished,' where '淫威' refers to excessive or tyrannical authority.
  • 不似从前之肆恣矣 - A contrast indicating that the person no longer acted as recklessly as before.
  • 旋以曹公问郡邑父老 - A phrase meaning 'immediately asked the elders of the county and town about the person,' where '旋' indicates immediacy.
  • 咸云实有其人，与其事 - A phrase meaning 'all said that the person and the events were real,' where '咸云' means 'all said.'
  • 至究何官属，同死者何人，死何月日 - A series of questions asking about the person's official position, the people who died with them, and the date of their death.
  • 昆山有无耳孙 - A question about whether there are descendants in Kunshan, where '耳孙' refers to distant descendants.
  • 不能得端的 - A phrase meaning 'could not get a definitive answer,' where '端的' means 'definite' or 'certain.'
  • 观察函询昆宰，亦无还云 - A phrase meaning 'the observer sent a letter to the magistrate of Kunshan, but there was no reply,' where '函询' means 'to inquire by letter.'
  • 府乘亦无纪载 - A phrase meaning 'the local gazetteer also did not record it,' where '府乘' refers to a local gazetteer.
  • 遽见烛跋，辞去 - A phrase meaning 'suddenly saw the candle wick burn out, and took leave,' where '烛跋' refers to the wick of a candle.
  • 晨即前赴省垣 - A phrase meaning 'in the morning, went to the provincial capital,' where '省垣' refers to the provincial capital.
  • 蓦忆昨宵所梦忠臣曹公者 - A phrase meaning 'suddenly remembered the loyal minister Cao Gong from the dream last night,' where '蓦忆' means 'suddenly remembered.'
  • 其即红袍纱帽，负手长吟者乎 - A question asking if the person in the dream was the same as the one wearing a red robe and a black hat, walking with hands behind his back and reciting poetry.
  • 玩其诗句 - A phrase meaning 'to ponder his verses,' where '玩' means 'to ponder' or 'to appreciate.'

  • 慎斋又为余述 - Means 'Mr. Shenzhai again told me,' indicating a narrative introduction.
  • 劳廉访任内 - Refers to the period when Mr. Lao was the censors-in-chief, a high-ranking official.
  • 雇女佣蔡妪 - Means 'hired a female servant named Lady Cai.'
  • 年将不惑 - A euphemism for 'around forty years old,' derived from the *Analects*.
  • 尚风致 - Means 'still had a graceful appearance.'
  • 居恒亦解供操作 - Means 'usually understood how to perform domestic tasks.'
  • 下榻不与人同舍 - Means 'slept in a separate room from others.'
  • 夜深有自窗外过者 - Means 'at night, someone passed by the window.'
  • 闻一人屋内语 - Means 'heard someone speaking inside the room.'
  • 细听之，则有亵昵声 - Means 'upon closer listening, there were intimate sounds.'
  • 疑遭狐魅 - Means 'suspected to be bewitched by a fox spirit.'
  • 不深异 - Means 'did not find it very strange.'
  • 署中时有抛砖掷瓦等患 - Means 'there were frequent incidents of throwing bricks and tiles.'
  • 匕鬯自飞于梁 - Means 'wine offerings flew onto the beams.'
  • 溲溺自集于盏 - Means 'urine and feces collected in cups.'
  • 虔祀之 - Means 'worshiped it devoutly.'
  • 而闹如故 - Means 'but the disturbances continued as before.'
  • 内眷纳凉 - Means 'the family members were cooling off.'
  • 忽见一黑衣人立蔡妪后 - Means 'suddenly saw a black-clothed person standing behind Lady Cai.'
  • 上下大哗 - Means 'everyone made a great commotion.'
  • 争逐之 - Means 'tried to chase it.'
  • 行甚速 - Means 'moved very quickly.'
  • 追莫及 - Means 'could not catch up.'
  • 而其恶闹，更无忌惮矣 - Means 'and the disturbances became even more brazen.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 桂林臬署 - Refers to the judicial office in Guilin, a city in southern China.
  • 慎斋 - A pseudonym for the narrator, likely a scholar or official.
  • 劳廉访 - Refers to Mr. Lao, a censors-in-chief, a high-ranking official responsible for judicial matters.
  • 狐魅 - A fox spirit, a common motif in Chinese folklore, often associated with mischievous or malevolent behavior.
  • 内眷 - Refers to the family members, particularly the women of the household.
  • 黑衣人 - A black-clothed person, often a symbol of mystery or supernatural presence in Chinese literature.

Proper Nouns

  • 桂林 - The city of Guilin, located in southern China.
  • 臬署 - The judicial office.
  • 慎斋 - The narrator's pseudonym.
  • 劳廉访 - Mr. Lao, the censors-in-chief.
  • 蔡妪 - Lady Cai, the female servant.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 旋有女巫 - Indicates a sudden appearance of a female witch.
  • 私泄于廉访之执事人云 - Refers to the act of confiding in someone in authority.
  • 闹者，山魈也 - Identifies the source of the disturbance as a shanxiao (mountain demon).
  • 向与蔡妪私 - Describes a past relationship with Lady Cai.
  • 初魅时，居然美少年 - Describes the initial appearance of the demon as a handsome young man.
  • 且时赠钱米博妪欢 - Indicates the demon's efforts to gain affection by giving money and rice.
  • 久之，渐露真形状 - Describes the change in appearance over time.
  • 独足，怪丑莫名 - Describes the demon's final form as one-legged and grotesquely ugly.
  • 然妪竟不能遣之去 - Indicates Lady Cai's inability to remove the demon.
  • 且与之狼狈 - Suggests a complicated relationship.
  • 凡佣主惮其闹，必托妪退送 - Describes the landlord's actions in entrusting Lady Cai to send the demon away.
  • 以是得财贿 - Indicates the financial gain from these actions.
  • 是妪也，淫而贪，虺蜴其心，魑魅其偶 - Describes Lady Cai's character as lustful, greedy, with a serpent's heart and a demon's companion.
  • 但不解煌煌戟，何能令若辈出入 - Expresses confusion about how the splendid halberd could allow such creatures to come and go.
  • 其不遭显戮者，真漏网之鱼哉 - Suggests that those who avoided punishment are escaped fish.
  • 此一异也 - Summarizes the first part of the story as one strange event.
  • 又署背倚城隍，且近山 - Describes the location as backed by the city god temple and close to the mountains.
  • 常山率然之流，缘卑湿而滋长，弗触之，无害也 - Describes the local wildlife as growing due to low and damp conditions, but harmless if not disturbed.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 堆假山 - Means 'piled up artificial hills,' describing the construction of artificial landscapes.

  • 大仅如盏 - Means 'as large as a cup,' indicating the size of the cave's entrance.

  • 填以土，旋启 - Means 'filled with soil, then opened,' describing the process of filling and uncovering the cave.
  • 中有巨蟒为之宅 - Means 'inside is a giant python that lives there,' indicating the presence of a large snake.
  • 蟒身作黄金色 - Means 'the python's body is golden-colored,' describing the color of the snake.
  • 顶有肉角寸许 - Means 'it has a fleshy horn about an inch long on its head,' describing a unique feature of the snake.
  • 色殷红 - Means 'the color is bright red,' describing the color of the fleshy horn.
  • 粗如桶 - Means 'as thick as a barrel,' describing the thickness of the snake's body.
  • 长十余丈 - Means 'over ten zhang long,' indicating the length of the snake.
  • 居恒不甚出 - Means 'it rarely comes out,' describing the snake's behavior.
  • 凡官有迁擢信，必预自榻后出 - Means 'whenever there is news of an official's promotion, it always comes out from behind the bed in advance,' describing the snake's predictive behavior.
  • 峨峨盘厅事中 - Means 'majestically coiled in the hall,' describing the snake's posture.
  • 目闪视人 - Means 'its eyes flash at people,' describing the snake's gaze.
  • 然绝无噬人意 - Means 'but it has no intention of biting people,' indicating the snake's non-aggressive nature.
  • 旋逡巡，仍自榻下没 - Means 'soon it cautiously retreats and disappears back under the bed,' describing the snake's return to hiding.
  • 出三日，官果迁 - Means 'three days later, the official indeed gets promoted,' confirming the snake's prediction.
  • 第榻后护壁，皆木板无隙 - Means 'but behind the bed, the wall panels are all wooden boards with no gaps,' describing the physical structure of the room.
  • 不知其从何出入也 - Means 'it is unknown how it enters and exits,' expressing the mystery of the snake's movements.
  • 顾身已蟒矣，犹知韬晦 - Means 'but even though it is a python, it still knows to keep a low profile,' describing the snake's wisdom.
  • 恐致雷诛 - Means 'fearing punishment by thunder,' indicating the snake's fear of divine retribution.
  • 迨官有莺迁，必预报鹊喜 - Means 'when an official is promoted, it will definitely foretell the good news,' describing the snake's continued predictive behavior.
  • 似有卫主之心 - Means 'it seems to have a heart to protect its master,' indicating the snake's loyalty.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 署 - Refers to an office or government building.
  • 客厅 - Refers to a hall or reception area.
  • 芭蕉 - Refers to banana trees, which are common in Chinese gardens.
  • 假山 - Refers to artificial hills or rockeries, a common feature in traditional Chinese gardens.
  • 巨蟒 - Refers to a large python, a type of snake.
  • 迁擢 - Refers to an official's promotion or elevation.
  • 榻 - Refers to a bed or couch.

  • 莺迁 - Refers to the promotion of an official, often symbolized by a bird moving to a higher perch.
  • 鹊喜 - Refers to the good news of an official's promotion, often symbolized by a crowing rooster.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sequence '署之客厅窗后，植芭蕉数本，堆假山，极嶙峋' describes the setting and must be read as a series of actions and descriptions.
  • The clause '山足土洞，口圆如井，大仅如盏，瞰之似不甚深' describes the cave and its features, and must be read as a single descriptive unit.
  • The clause '填以土，旋启，中有巨蟒为之宅' describes the discovery of the snake and must be read as a sequence of actions.
  • The clause '蟒身作黄金色，顶有肉角寸许，色殷红，粗如桶，长十余丈' describes the snake's appearance and must be read as a series of descriptive phrases.
  • The clause '居恒不甚出，凡官有迁擢信，必预自榻后出，峨峨盘厅事中，目闪视人，然绝无噬人意' describes the snake's behavior and must be read as a sequence of actions and observations.
  • The clause '旋逡巡，仍自榻下没' describes the snake's return to hiding and must be read as a sequence of actions.
  • The clause '出三日，官果迁' confirms the snake's prediction and must be read as a sequence of events.
  • The clause '第榻后护壁，皆木板无隙，不知其从何出入也' describes the mystery of the snake's movements and must be read as a single descriptive unit.
  • The clause '顾身已蟒矣，犹知韬晦，恐致雷诛' describes the snake's wisdom and fear and must be read as a single descriptive unit.
  • The clause '迨官有莺迁，必预报鹊喜，似有卫主之心' describes the snake's continued predictive behavior and loyalty and must be read as a single descriptive unit.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 烈殇尽孝 - A title combining '烈' (heroic), '殇' (bereaved), and '尽孝' (filial piety), suggesting a story of heroic filial devotion in the face of bereavement.
  • 河间西鄙 - Refers to a remote or border area west of Hejian (modern-day Hengshui, Hebei Province).
  • 传者忘其姓，仅得其小字也 - Indicates that the narrator forgot the woman's surname but only remembers her nickname, implying a focus on her personal story rather than her family lineage.
  • 红羊之岁 - A euphemism for the year 1644, a time of great turmoil and famine in Chinese history, often associated with the fall of the Ming Dynasty and the rise of the Qing.
  • 北直大饥 - Refers to a major famine in the northern part of the direct-controlled territories (Beizhili), a region under direct imperial control.
  • 道途枕饿殍 - A vivid image of the road being littered with the corpses of the starving, emphasizing the severity of the famine.
  • 土人易子析骸 - Describes the desperate act of people swapping their children for food or even resorting to cannibalism, highlighting the extreme conditions.
  • 贾人某 - Refers to a merchant, whose name is omitted, indicating that the focus is on the merchant's actions rather than his identity.
  • 以武庠习负贩 - Indicates that the merchant learned the trade of transporting goods through military schools, suggesting a background in both commerce and martial training.
  • 妻素悍，借以逃阃威 - Describes the merchant's wife as typically domineering, and that he used his business to escape her harshness, implying a strained marital relationship.
  • 亡夫巡检某公也 - Refers to the deceased husband, a local official (巡检), whose name is omitted, focusing on his role rather than his identity.
  • 家赤贫 - Describes the family as extremely poor, emphasizing their dire economic situation.
  • 生者固饥，死者亦盼断麦饭 - Describes the living as hungry and the dead as having been deprived of even a simple funeral meal, highlighting the family's poverty.
  • 女子千金耶 - A rhetorical question asking if the girl is a precious treasure, emphasizing her value to her mother.
  • 年十六，至今未字 - Indicates that the girl is sixteen years old and has not yet been married, emphasizing her youth and unmarried status.
  • 未亡人设旦夕殒霜露 - Describes the mother's fear of dying soon, leaving her daughter without support, emphasizing her vulnerability.
  • 诚不知一块肉将安归 - A metaphorical expression suggesting that the mother does not know where her daughter will go if she dies, emphasizing her concern for her daughter's future.
  • 贾亦代唏嘘 - Indicates that the merchant also felt moved to tears, showing his emotional response to the mother's plight.
  • 乃探囊出白镪十两授媪 - Describes the merchant taking out ten taels of silver and giving it to the old woman, showing his generosity.
  • 坚却之 - Indicates that the old woman firmly refused the money, showing her reluctance to accept charity.
  • 乘不意，蓦弃破几上 - Describes the merchant taking advantage of the old woman's distraction to leave the money on the broken table, showing his cunning.
  • 而飞骑兔脱 - Describes the merchant riding away quickly, like a rabbit escaping, showing his haste to leave.
  • 母子追莫及 - Indicates that the mother and daughter could not catch up to the merchant, showing their inability to thank him.
  • 遥遥叩拜 - Describes the mother and daughter bowing from a distance, showing their gratitude and respect.
  • 怜孤寡 - Refers to the merchant's compassion for the orphaned and widowed, showing his charitable nature.
  • 有古侠士风 - Describes the merchant as having the spirit of an ancient knight-errant, showing his heroic qualities.
  • 实不知其垂涎于二八娇娃也 - A critical remark suggesting that the merchant was actually lusting after the beautiful sixteen-year-old girl, showing his ulterior motives.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 越半载 - Means 'after half a year,' indicating a passage of time.
  • 归装更累累 - Means 'the return load is even heavier,' suggesting increased wealth or possessions.
  • 无恙 - A polite phrase meaning 'safe and well,' often used to inquire about someone's health or safety.
  • 微长者 - Means 'without the kind gentleman,' expressing gratitude and dependence.
  • 北邙山枯鱼肆 - A metaphorical expression referring to a place of death or despair, often used in Chinese literature to symbolize a grim fate.
  • 乡贯姓氏 - Refers to one's hometown, native place, and surname, important for identifying and addressing people in traditional Chinese society.
  • 俾供养酬大德 - Means 'to provide support and repay great kindness,' indicating a desire to show gratitude.
  • 戋戋者 - A humble term meaning 'a small amount' or 'a little,' often used to downplay one's own contributions.
  • 挂齿颊 - Means 'to mention' or 'to talk about,' often used in a modest or dismissive way.
  • 具鸡黍供客 - A humble expression meaning 'to prepare a simple meal for guests,' often used to show hospitality.
  • 雀屏选 - A metaphorical expression referring to selecting a spouse, often used in the context of marriage.
  • 招子说 - Refers to a traditional practice or saying related to marriage or adoption.
  • 白金五十两 - Means 'fifty taels of silver,' a unit of currency in traditional Chinese society.
  • 屈柳姑作螟蛉 - Means 'to adopt the young willow girl as a son,' referring to the practice of adoption in traditional Chinese society.
  • 一索得男 - Means 'to have a son on the first try,' referring to the traditional desire for a male heir.
  • 乘龙婿 - A metaphorical expression referring to a noble or distinguished husband.
  • 屈 - Means 'to adopt' or 'to accept,' often used in the context of adoption or marriage.
  • 弱息凌夷 - Means 'a weak and declining child,' referring to someone who is frail or in poor health.
  • 宦裔 - Refers to someone from an official or scholarly family, often used to describe a person of high social status.
  • 继膝下固佳 - Means 'to follow in the footsteps of the father and be good,' referring to the desire for a son to continue the family line.
  • 下充妾媵 - Means 'to serve as a concubine or secondary wife,' referring to the practice of concubinage in traditional Chinese society.
  • 明言于其先 - Means 'to clearly state to the ancestors,' referring to the practice of communicating with ancestors in traditional Chinese society.
  • 何言欤 - A rhetorical question meaning 'what is this saying?' or 'what is this saying about?' often used to express surprise or disbelief.
  • 天日可鉴 - Means 'may heaven and earth witness,' a solemn oath or promise.
  • 设信誓 - Means 'to make a solemn promise,' often used in the context of a vow or oath.
  • 粲粲者在是 - Means 'the shining ones are here,' referring to the silver or wealth being offered.
  • 请姥暂割爱 - Means 'please let the old woman temporarily part with her love,' referring to the request to adopt the daughter.
  • 耽延 - Means 'to delay' or 'to postpone,' often used in the context of waiting or hesitating.
  • 闺中望夫山 - A metaphorical expression referring to the longing of a wife for her husband, often used in the context of marriage.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 女恐过恸伤母心 - The daughter feared that excessive grief would hurt her mother's heart. This reflects cultural sensitivity to emotional restraint, especially in maternal relationships.
  • 即忍痛拜别 - She immediately endured the pain and bid farewell. This conveys the daughter's emotional struggle and her decision to suppress her grief.
  • 贾授以鞭 - Jia gave her a whip. This may carry symbolic meaning, such as a tool for control or a sign of authority.
  • 联辔就道 - They rode together on the road. This may symbolize a shared journey or fate.
  • 行里许 - After traveling a few miles. This may imply a brief period of time or a specific point in the journey.
  • 女耳中犹仿佛阿娘唤女声 - The daughter still faintly heard her mother's voice calling her. This captures the daughter's lingering connection to her mother, even in her absence.
  • 遂大恸 - She then wept bitterly. This conveys the daughter's emotional release, but may also reflect cultural norms around public displays of grief.
  • 贾告以不能两全 - Jia told her that it was impossible to have both. This introduces a moral or practical dilemma, suggesting that the daughter must choose between her mother and her own path.
  • 再三抚慰 - He comforted her repeatedly. This conveys Jia's attempt to console the daughter, but may also reflect the cultural expectation of male authority and paternal care.
  • 抵腰站 - They arrived at a place called Yauzhan. This may carry historical or cultural significance, such as a specific location or a symbolic place.
  • 置女逆旅 - He left the daughter at an inn. This may imply her isolation or vulnerability.
  • 距恩仅三十里 - It was only thirty miles from En. This may symbolize the daughter's proximity to her mother or her own destiny.
  • 忽迟疑不发 - Suddenly, he hesitated and did not proceed. This conveys Jia's indecision or reluctance, but may also reflect a moral or practical dilemma.
  • 询若何 - He asked what was the matter. This reflects the cultural expectation of questioning or seeking clarification.
  • 此间有故人招饮，且有交易勾当耳 - There is an old acquaintance who invited me to drink, and there are some business matters to attend to. This reflects the cultural importance of social obligations and business dealings.
  • 地多妓 - The place is full of courtesans. This reflects the cultural norms around gender and sexuality.
  • 女不知也 - The daughter did not know. This conveys the daughter's ignorance or naivety, but may also reflect her isolation or lack of experience.
  • 日则静掩双柴 - During the day, she quietly closed the double doors. This may reflect her loneliness or self-imposed isolation.
  • 夕则一灯闪壁 - At night, a single lamp flickered on the wall. This creates a vivid image of the daughter's living conditions, but may also reflect her emotional state or the passage of time.
  • 鹃啼血洒，蝶梦魂飞 - The cuckoo's cry was like blood being shed, and the butterfly's dream was like a soul flying away. This conveys the daughter's emotional turmoil, but may also reflect cultural symbolism around nature and the supernatural.
  • 邻家马二娘，虽时来闲话 - The neighbor, Ma Ernang, would occasionally come to chat. This reflects the daughter's limited social circle or her need for companionship.
  • 而忆母之心终不释 - But her longing for her mother never diminished. This conveys the daughter's persistent emotional connection to her mother, but may also reflect cultural norms around filial piety.
  • 媪自送女出门，愈无聊赖 - The old woman, after sending the daughter out, felt increasingly aimless. This may reflect her sense of loss or responsibility.
  • 纵阿堵能疗眼前疮 - Even if money could cure her current pain. This may reflect the old woman's financial concerns or her sense of helplessness.
  • 而掌珠已失心头肉 - But her cherished daughter had become like a piece of her heart. This conveys the old woman's emotional pain, but may also reflect cultural symbolism around family and love.
  • 昕夕泪雨，渐绝水浆 - She wept tears day and night, gradually losing her appetite for food and drink. This may reflect the cultural expectation of grief and mourning.
  • 越七日夕 - After seven days. This may symbolize a specific period of mourning or reflection.
  • 正呻吟病榻间 - She was groaning in bed. This may reflect her emotional or psychological state.
  • 忽闻女子叩门声 - Suddenly, she heard the sound of a woman knocking on the door. This may reflect the old woman's heightened sense of awareness or anticipation.
  • 媪颤声答云 - The old woman answered in a trembling voice. This conveys the old woman's emotional state, but may also reflect her fear or uncertainty.
  • 此穷窟也，谁家女娇生，来乞水火？ - This is a poor hovel, who is this pampered girl, coming to beg for water and fire? This may reflect the old woman's skepticism or surprise.
  • 应曰 - She replied. This may reflect the old woman's curiosity or confusion.
  • 儿声音，娘不识耶？柳归也？ - Is it my child's voice, mother doesn't recognize it? Is it Liu Pu returning? This may reflect the old woman's emotional state or her sense of disbelief.
  • 媪惊喜，强起拔键 - The old woman was surprised and delighted, forcing herself to get up and unlock the door. This may reflect her emotional or physical state.
  • 奔入，果不诬 - She rushed in, and indeed it was not a lie. This confirms the old woman's discovery, but may also reflect her sense of relief or joy.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 冉冉拜灯下 - 冉冉是副词，意为‘慢慢地’或‘逐渐地’。‘拜灯下’意为‘在灯下鞠躬’，可以理解为‘在昏暗的灯光下鞠躬’或‘在灯光前鞠躬’。
  • 母心慰否 - ‘母心慰’意为‘母亲的心得到安慰’，‘否’是疑问词，意为‘吗’。
  • 得毋鬼耶 - ‘得毋’是情态词，意为‘难道’或‘或许’，‘鬼’意为‘鬼魂’。
  • 抑梦寐耶 - ‘抑’是情态词，意为‘还是’，‘梦寐’意为‘梦境’。
  • 渠携儿去 - ‘渠’是第三人称代词，意为‘他’，‘携’意为‘带着’，‘儿’意为‘孩子’。
  • 家中生菩萨 - ‘生菩萨’是比喻表达，意为‘活菩萨’或‘圣人’。
  • 妒虎也 - ‘妒虎’是比喻表达，意为‘嫉妒的老虎’或‘嫉妒的野兽’。
  • 疑其纳小星 - ‘疑’意为‘怀疑’，‘其’是第三人称代词，意为‘他’，‘纳’意为‘接纳’或‘接受’，‘小星’意为‘小星星’或‘小妾’。
  • 私以一骑送儿归 - ‘私’意为‘私下’，‘以’意为‘用’，‘一骑’意为‘一匹马’，‘送儿归’意为‘送孩子回家’。
  • 执鞭人已就前村宿耳 - ‘执鞭人’意为‘持鞭的人’（可能是骑马的人），‘已就’意为‘已经去’，‘前村’意为‘前面的村庄’，‘宿耳’意为‘过夜’。
  • 媪庆珠还 - ‘媪’意为‘老妇人’（母亲），‘庆’意为‘庆祝’，‘珠还’是比喻表达，意为‘珍珠归还’（指失物或人归来）。
  • 沉疴顿已 - ‘沉疴’意为‘重病’，‘顿已’意为‘突然痊愈’。
  • 女虽璧返 - ‘女’意为‘女孩’，‘虽’意为‘虽然’，‘璧返’是比喻表达，意为‘像玉璧一样归来’（指珍贵之物归来）。
  • 远山常颦 - ‘远山’意为‘远方的山’，‘常颦’意为‘经常皱眉’（比喻持续的悲伤或忧虑）。
  • 顾貌如故 - ‘顾’意为‘但是’，‘貌如故’意为‘容貌依旧’。
  • 音如故 - ‘音如故’意为‘声音依旧’。
  • 而艺增且绝 - ‘而’意为‘但是’，‘艺增’意为‘技艺增加’，‘且绝’意为‘而且卓越’。
  • 人皆惘惘 - ‘人皆’意为‘人人’，‘惘惘’意为‘迷茫’或‘困惑’。
  • 自刺绣以至书画词赋 - ‘自’意为‘从’，‘刺绣’意为‘刺绣’，‘以至’意为‘到’，‘书画’意为‘书画’，‘词赋’意为‘诗词’。
  • 无一不精 - ‘无一不’意为‘没有一个不是’，‘精’意为‘精通’。
  • 每出手制 - ‘每’意为‘每次’，‘出手’意为‘出手’，‘制’意为‘作品’。
  • 浼村人寄通都 - ‘浼’意为‘委托’，‘村人’意为‘村民’，‘寄’意为‘寄送’，‘通都’意为‘大城市’。
  • 售最速而价亦丰 - ‘售最速’意为‘销售最快’，‘价亦丰’意为‘价格也高’。
  • 不书柳也 - ‘不书’意为‘不写’，‘柳’是人名，可能是女孩的原名。
  • 由是供饔餐 - ‘由是’意为‘因此’，‘供’意为‘提供’，‘饔餐’意为‘每日的饮食’。
  • 无事采薇矣 - ‘无事’意为‘没有事情’，‘采薇’意为‘采摘野菜’（比喻贫穷的生活）。
  • 葺庐舍 - ‘葺’意为‘修缮’，‘庐舍’意为‘茅舍’。
  • 无事牵萝矣 - ‘无事’意为‘没有事情’，‘牵萝’意为‘绑藤蔓’（比喻简陋的住所）。

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 冉冉拜灯下 - 在灯下鞠躬的情景暗示了尊敬或感激的情绪，可能是在家庭环境中。
  • 母心慰否 - 这反映了母亲与孩子之间深厚的情感纽带，这是古典中国文学中的常见主题。
  • 得毋鬼耶 - 提到鬼魂反映了中国文化中对超自然生物的信仰以及对未知的恐惧。
  • 抑梦寐耶 - 提到梦境强调了现实与幻想之间的模糊界限，这是古典中国思想中的一个重要概念。
  • 家中生菩萨 - ‘活菩萨’的比喻暗示了一个具有极高德行或神圣品质的人。
  • 妒虎也 - ‘嫉妒的老虎’的比喻暗示了强烈的嫉妒或怨恨。
  • 疑其纳小星 - 提到‘小星星’（小妾）反映了古代中国家庭中的复杂社会关系。
  • 私以一骑送儿归 - 私下送孩子回家的行为表明了急切和关怀。
  • 执鞭人已就前村宿耳 - 骑马的人在前面的村庄过夜，表明了旅途的漫长和需要休息。
  • 媪庆珠还 - ‘珍珠归还’的比喻象征着失物或人归来的喜悦。
  • 沉疴顿已 - 重病的突然痊愈反映了人们对奇迹康复的信仰。
  • 女虽璧返 - ‘像玉璧一样归来’的比喻强调了女孩的价值和珍贵。
  • 远山常颦 - ‘远方的山经常皱眉’的比喻暗示了持续的悲伤或忧虑。
  • 顾貌如故 - 女孩的外貌没有改变，反映了外表的恒定尽管内心有所变化。
  • 音如故 - 女孩的声音没有改变，进一步强调了外表的恒定。
  • 而艺增且绝 - 她的艺术技能增加且卓越，突出了她非凡的才能。
  • 人皆惘惘 - 他人的困惑或迷失反映了她转变的影响。
  • 自刺绣以至书画词赋 - 从刺绣到书法、绘画、诗词的广泛艺术才能显示了她的多才多艺。
  • 无一不精 - 她在所有这些领域都精通，强调了她卓越的技能。
  • 每出手制 - 她定期创作高质量的作品，表明了她的敬业精神和才华。
  • 浼村人寄通都 - 委托村民将她的作品寄往大城市，表明了她日益增长的声誉和作品的传播。
  • 售最速而价亦丰 - 她的作品销售迅速且价格高昂，反映了她的受欢迎程度和价值。
  • 不书柳也 - 决定不使用原名表明了她生活中的重大变化。
  • 由是供饔餐 - 提供每日饮食反映了她新生活的稳定和舒适。
  • 无事采薇矣 - 不再需要采摘野菜表明了更舒适和安全的生活方式。
  • 葺庐舍 - 修缮茅舍表明了一种持久性和居住条件的改善。
  • 无事牵萝矣 - 不再需要绑藤蔓表明了更稳定和舒适的住所。

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 俟吾母百年 - Euphemistic way of saying 'until my mother passes away' or 'until my mother dies.'
  • 终当求牡 - Means 'eventually seek a husband' or 'will eventually look for a husband.'
  • 岂竟守雌 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'how can I remain unmarried forever?' '守雌' refers to remaining unmarried, often used in the context of women.
  • 泪涔涔下 - Describes tears flowing continuously, indicating deep sorrow.
  • 浩浩之水，育育之鱼 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'vast waters and abundant fish,' often used to describe a situation where one feels they have no chance or opportunity.
  • 毕生无分矣 - Means 'no chance for life' or 'no hope for life,' indicating a sense of despair.
  • 阿娘苦汝 - Phrase expressing maternal love and concern, meaning 'Mother is suffering for you' or 'Mother is worried about you.'
  • 自有安乐窝 - Means 'has its own place of peace and comfort,' often used to describe the afterlife.
  • 身外身，影外影也 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'a body without a body, a shadow without a shadow,' indicating a sense of non-existence or detachment from the physical world.
  • 以生为死，以死为生 - Paradoxical expression meaning 'to treat life as death and death as life,' indicating a philosophical or spiritual perspective on life and death.
  • 散给茕独辈 - Means 'distribute to the lonely and destitute,' referring to helping those who are alone and in need.
  • 烦代唪一声佛 - Means 'please recite a Buddhist prayer for me,' asking for a favor in the context of Buddhist practice.
  • 祝我母早升天 - Means 'pray for my mother to ascend to heaven soon,' expressing a wish for the deceased to reach a better place.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 聘为妇 - Refers to the traditional Chinese practice of marriage, specifically the act of a man seeking to marry a woman.
  • 媪 - Refers to an elderly woman, often used to describe a mother or grandmother.
  • 阿娘 - Term of endearment for mother, commonly used in Chinese culture.
  • 地下 - Refers to the afterlife or the underworld, often used in the context of death and the deceased.
  • 茕独 - Refers to those who are alone and destitute, often used to describe the poor and lonely.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 佛声震山谷 - A vivid description of the sound of the Buddha's voice shaking the mountains and valleys, likely a metaphor for a powerful or awe-inspiring event.
  • 焚所居成白地 - Literally means 'burning the residence to turn it into a white land,' which can be interpreted as a complete destruction or purification of the place.
  • 惨与众别 - A poignant farewell, emphasizing the emotional weight of the separation.
  • 翩然抵旧处 - Describes a graceful or sudden arrival at an old place.
  • 仍宿当日之逆旅 - Refers to staying at the same inn as on a previous occasion.
  • 主人目之，大骇 - The innkeeper looks at the person and is greatly startled.
  • 何酷似十年前之柳姑也 - Asks how similar the person is to a woman named Liu Bo Gu from ten years ago.
  • 虎贲貌似中郎耳 - A response indicating that the person resembles a military officer (虎贲) who looks like a middle-ranking official (中郎).
  • 泫然以杯酌地 - Weeps and pours wine on the ground as a gesture of remembrance or offering.
  • 贞洁女儿花 - A metaphor for a chaste and virtuous young woman.
  • 安能污狭斜 - Asks how such a pure woman could be tainted by the narrow and crooked paths (likely referring to immoral or corrupt environments).
  • 殒身遭毒鸨 - Describes a tragic death due to the treachery of a wicked woman (毒鸨).
  • 炼魂慰慈鸦 - Refers to the purification of the soul to comfort a kind bird (慈鸦), possibly a metaphor for a kind or gentle spirit.
  • 不遇奸人赚 - Indicates that the person did not encounter a deceitful person.
  • 何邀上帝夸 - Asks why the person would invite praise from God.
  • 可怜残月底，《折柳》唱无家 - A poignant image of a lonely person under the moon, singing a song of farewell (折柳) and feeling homeless.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 佛声震山谷 - The sound of the Buddha's voice shaking the mountains and valleys is a common metaphor in Chinese literature, often used to describe a powerful or divine presence.
  • 焚所居成白地 - Burning the residence to turn it into a white land can be seen as a ritual purification or a symbol of complete destruction.
  • 惨与众别 - The emotional weight of the farewell suggests a deep bond or a significant loss.
  • 翩然抵旧处 - The graceful or sudden arrival at an old place may refer to a return to a familiar or significant location.
  • 仍宿当日之逆旅 - Staying at the same inn as on a previous occasion suggests a return to a past experience or a sense of continuity.
  • 主人目之，大骇 - The innkeeper's reaction indicates that the person is recognizable or notable.
  • 何酷似十年前之柳姑也 - The comparison to a woman from ten years ago suggests a long-standing memory or a significant figure in the past.
  • 虎贲貌似中郎耳 - The resemblance to a military officer and a middle-ranking official may refer to a respected or influential person.
  • 泫然以杯酌地 - Pouring wine on the ground as a gesture of remembrance is a traditional Chinese practice.
  • 贞洁女儿花 - The metaphor for a chaste and virtuous young woman reflects traditional Chinese values of purity and virtue.
  • 安能污狭斜 - The reference to narrow and crooked paths (狭斜) may allude to immoral or corrupt environments, often associated with brothels or other disreputable places.
  • 殒身遭毒鸨 - The tragic death due to the treachery of a wicked woman (毒鸨) may refer to a specific historical or literary event.
  • 炼魂慰慈鸦 - The purification of the soul to comfort a kind bird (慈鸦) may be a metaphor for a kind or gentle spirit, possibly referring to a deceased loved one.
  • 不遇奸人赚 - The avoidance of deceitful people suggests a life of integrity and honesty.
  • 何邀上帝夸 - The question about inviting praise from God may reflect a sense of humility or a lack of need for external validation.
  • 可怜残月底，《折柳》唱无家 - The image of a lonely person under the moon, singing a song of farewell (折柳), and feeling homeless may be a reference to a specific poem or a common theme in Chinese literature.
